research.summary: The Joon Shop is an established solid perfume brand that positions itself as a luxurious yet mindful daily self-care ritual, blending Korean formulation expertise with a premium, natural ingredient focus. Its identity centers on compact, alcohol-free, vegan solid perfumes that feel elegant, tactile, and intimate, targeting customers who value quiet luxury and safe, high-quality formulations.
competitors: Blur India (solid perfumes, e.g., Smells Like An Autumn Morning)
Kama Ayurveda (natural, Ayurvedic beauty and wellness)
Sol de Janeiro (sensory-rich, fragrance-forward body care)

palette.role: neutral accent / shadows, subtle UI elements
soft accent referencing natural ingredients / calming green
luxury highlight accent (subtle brushed-gold tone for details and props)

research.aesthetic: Backgrounds and surfaces are predominantly high-key and neutral: seamless matte white (#FFFFFF), warm off-white (#F5F5DC), and light stone or marble-like textures, with occasional light wood or soft linen to signal natural, tactile luxury; products are often placed on clean planes with gentle shadows rather than busy environments. The colour grade is clean, slightly desaturated, and low-contrast, keeping whites soft rather than stark, skin tones (if present) warm and natural, and accent colours limited to muted greens and soft golds that echo the brand palette; overall, images feel calm and airy rather than vibrant. Styling is minimal to medium density: hero shots are mostly product-only (compacts alone or in simple stacks), with a few carefully chosen props like citrus slices, single flower stems, dried petals, smooth stones, or a simple ceramic tray; props are used sparingly to hint at fragrance notes and self-care rituals without clutter. Lighting is soft, diffused, and daylight-mimicking, typically from one side (45° window light or softbox) with gentle falloff and faint, soft-edged shadows, sometimes with a slight back or top-back kicker to reveal texture on the balm surface; no harsh specular highlights or deep contrast. Compositions favour centered hero products, clean flat-lays, and simple three-quarter angles, with plenty of negative space and crops that keep the product clearly legible (rarely cutting through the logo or main compact face); depth-of-field is moderate, with product in crisp focus and backgrounds gently blurred when shooting lifestyle. The feed is primarily product-only images with occasional hand shots or partial body crops to show application, but not model-driven fashion storytelling; faces, if used, are secondary to the compact and gesture of use.
